perturbation.original_text: iPass may change the pricing in Exhibit C upon notice to Channel Partner, such changes to be effective within thirty (30) days after the date of such notice, such changes may include, by way of example only, the application of end of life fees.
perturbation.changed_text: iPass may change the pricing in Exhibit C upon notice to Channel Partner, such changes to be effective immediately after the date of such notice, such changes may include, by way of example only, the application of end of life fees.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for changes to the pricing in Exhibit C with a 30-day notice period. The modified text states these changes are effective immediately. This causes an in-text contradiction because Channel Partner could claim that any price change requires a 30-day notice period as initially agreed, leading to conflict and ambiguity. This contradicts with Section 3.2, which states the payment terms, that are not immediate, but are on a monthly basis
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Payment. All payments hereunder will be in US Dollars and are due and payable within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice. iPass may terminate or modify these payment terms with reasonable notice, when, in its reasonable discretion, iPass believes that its payments may be at risk. All rights of Channel Partner are expressly made conditional upon timely payment, without the right of set off, of all amounts due by Channel Partner under the Agreement. Without limiting iPass' other available rights and remedies, iPass reserves the right to suspend or to terminate any Services ten (10) days after sending written notice of a payment that is thirty (30) or more days past due. iPass may include on an invoice, and Channel Partner agrees to pay for: (i) any Services used within one hundred eighty (180) days before the date of the invoice; and (ii) any corrections to amounts invoiced within one hundred eighty (180) days after the date of the original invoice. If Channel Partner reasonably disputes an invoiced amount, Channel Partner may: (a) withhold such amount from Channel Partner's payment, provided that Channel Partner gives iPass written notice of the dispute and the reason for such dispute within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice, and pays all invoiced amounts not subject to such dispute by the due date; or (b) in the event that the invoiced amount has already been paid in full, submit a claim to iPass within sixty (60) days following the date of the original invoice. The parties will cause their authorized representatives to meet within fifteen (15) days after Channel Partner's notice of dispute to negotiate in good faith to resolve the dispute. If such representatives have not resolved the dispute within fifteen (15) days after their first meeting, the parties will either decide mutually to continue negotiations or, if either party does not desire to continue negotiations, each party may exercise its available remedies with respect to the disputed amounts. To the extent a dispute resolution requires payment of disputed amounts, Channel Partner shall pay such disputed amounts promptly after the dispute is resolved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the effective date of pricing changes from 'thirty (30) days after the date of such notice' to 'immediately after the date of such notice.' This directly contradicts Section 3.2, which states that 'payments hereunder will be in US Dollars and are due and payable within thirty (30) days after the date of invoice.' The immediate effective date of pricing changes conflicts with the existing 30-day payment term, creating an in-text contradiction regarding when the Channel Partner is obligated to pay the new pricing.

perturbation.original_text: Channel Partner will be responsible for providing End Users Second Level Technical Support. Second Level Technical Support personnel who have received training regarding the Service and the Software from iPass pursuant to Section 2 of the Agreement will be authorized to contact the applicable Third Level Technical Support centers at iPass to resolve any problems that cannot be resolved by First Level Technical Support or Second Level Technical Support.
perturbation.changed_text: Channel Partner may choose whether or not to provide End Users Second Level Technical Support. Second Level Technical Support personnel who have received training regarding the Service and the Software from iPass pursuant to Section 2 of the Agreement will be authorized to contact the applicable Third Level Technical Support centers at iPass to resolve any problems that cannot be resolved by First Level Technical Support or Second Level Technical Support.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will be responsible' to 'may choose whether or not to provide', the contract now contains conflicting instructions as other sections assume the Channel Partner provides this support. This contradiction creates uncertainty on the responsibility of Channel Partner when providing end user support and what iPass is responsible on providing directly for the end user.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 iPass Support Services. iPass will provide Help Desk to Help Desk support to Channel Partner on components of the iPass Service e.g. the iPass Open Mobile Portal, Hosted Authentication Infrastructure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text makes providing Second Level Technical Support optional for the Channel Partner, whereas the contradicted text in section 4.2 assumes that iPass provides support to the Channel Partner, implying that the Channel Partner is providing support to the end-users. By making the Channel Partner's support optional, it introduces a contradiction because the iPass' support is designed to support the Channel Partner, therefore the iPass support becomes unclear and the Channel Partner's obligation is uncertain, thus creating a contradiction.
